chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Application No. S- 149 of 2022 (Abdul Latif Chohan v. The State) For the Applicant: Applicant Abdul Latif through Mr. Achar Khan Gabol, Advocate a/w applicant. For the Complainant: Mr. Faisal Shams, Advocate along with complainant. For the State: Syed Sardar Ali Shah, Additional P.G. Date(s) of Hearing: 06-02-2023 Date of Order: 06-02-2023 O R D E R Zafar Ahmed Rajput, J. - Having been rejected his earlier Crl. Pre-Arrest Bail Application No.265 of 2022 by learned Additional Sessions Judge-V, Sukkur vide order dated 12.03.2022, the applicant through instant Crl. Bail Application seeks the same relief from this Court in Crime No. 11 of 2022, registered at P.S, Abad-Sukkur, under Section 489-F PPC. He was admitted to interim pre-arrest bail by this Court vide order dated 01.04.2022, now the matter is fixed for confirmation of the same or otherwise. 2. At the very outside, learned counsel for the complainant states that the parties have settled their dispute outside the Court, hence he has no objection for the confirmation of interim pre-arrest bail already granted to the applicant by this Court. 3. Considering the fact that the alleged offence being punishable with imprisonment for three years does not fall within the prohibitory clause of Section 497 Cr.P.C; Challan has already been submitted by the police before the trial Court and parties have settled their dispute outside the Court, interim pre- Crl. B.A.No.S-149 of 2022 Page 2 of 2 arrest bail earlier granted to the applicant by this Court, vide order dated 01.04.2022, is hereby confirmed on same terms and conditions. 4.

Considering the fact that the alleged offence being punishable with imprisonment for three years does not fall within the prohibitory clause of Section 497 Cr.P.C; Challan has already been submitted by the police before the trial Court and parties have settled their dispute outside the Court, interim pre- Crl. B.A.No.S-149 of 2022 Page 2 of 2 arrest bail earlier granted to the applicant by this Court, vide order dated 01.04.2022, is hereby confirmed on same terms and conditions. 4. Needless to mention here that the observations made herein-above are tentative in nature and would not influence the trial Court while deciding the case of applicant on merits and if the applicant in any manner tries to misuse the concession of bail, it would be open for the trial Court to cancel the same after issuing him the requisite notice. The instant Crl. Bail Application stands allowed in above terms. J U D G E Ahmad
